task.title: Reject exact expected version zero before append
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/394 by rejecting ExpectedVersion::Exact(0) as an invalid caller expectation before payload encoding or storage access.
acceptance_criteria: Exact expected versions begin at one, while NoStream remains the only valid expectation for a missing stream.
Exact(0) returns EventLogError::InvalidExpectedVersion before payload encoding and exposes no underlying source.
The rejected append writes no row and stored-data validation remains covered independently.
The public event-log documentation describes the valid expected-version domain and stable error.
The pinned local and pull-request quality gates pass.

attempts.summary: Added a focused behavior test with an event serializer that panics if encoding runs.
attempts.diagnostic: The RED test failed to compile because EventLogError had no InvalidExpectedVersion variant, confirming that Exact(0) still fell through to generic expected-version handling.

attempts.summary: Added an append preflight guard and a dedicated source-less caller-domain error.
attempts.patch: EventLog::append now returns EventLogError::InvalidExpectedVersion(0) before consulting the event discriminator, payload version, serializer, or SQLite transaction.

attempts.summary: Ran the complete gate and found that an older corruption test used Exact(0) to reach stored-version validation.
attempts.diagnostic: The existing rejects_zero_as_the_current_stored_version_without_writing test received the new caller error before reading the intentionally corrupted row.
attempts.patch: Changed that test to use NoStream, which remains a valid caller expectation and still proves invalid stored current versions are rejected before concurrency matching.

attempts.summary: Verified the focused behavior, full repository gate, independent review, and required pull-request checks through merge.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused test and just verify passed, independent Codex review found no blocking issue, CodeRabbit generated no actionable comments, Rust quality gate run 29320633657 passed, and pull request 395 squash-merged as e238cb2.
attempts.patch: No corrective patch was required after review.

outcome.summary: Append now rejects Exact(0) before encoding as an explicit invalid caller expectation, preserves the stream, and keeps stored corruption distinct from caller validation.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test event_log rejects_exact_expected_version_zero_before_encoding -- --exact
Independent Codex pre-commit review for pull request 395: no blocking findings
GitHub Rust quality gate run 29320633657 for pull request 395
CodeRabbit review for pull request 395: review completed with no actionable comments and required status passed
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- record validate corpus/development/0018-exact-zero-expected-version.json # result: valid development record
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- corpus inspect corpus/development # result: 18 valid, 0 invalid

lessons: Represent a missing stream with NoStream rather than overloading numeric version zero; invalid domain values deserve an explicit caller error.
Validate caller-controlled expectation state before event encoding so deterministic input errors cannot be masked by serializer failures or storage access.
When a new preflight validation changes error precedence, update corruption tests to use a valid caller input that still reaches the persisted-data boundary.
A serializer that deliberately panics is a concise regression probe for proving validation occurs before encoding.
